email.style_instructions: TL;DR style. Open with a 2-3 item 'Top Picks' highlighting the most novel/important papers of the day. Then themed sections (~5). For each paper: a one-line takeaway, then 1-2 sentences on why it matters and what is genuinely novel. Flag [open-weights] releases explicitly and include model/code/GitHub links when available. Every paper links to its arXiv abstract or source. Keep it skimmable; no filler. If it was a thin day, a shorter digest is fine.

evaluation.novelty_criteria: Prefer genuinely new ideas: new problem framings, architectures, training objectives, theoretical results, or surprising empirical findings. Down-rank minor tweaks to training protocols, incremental benchmark bumps, routine scaling reports, and dataset/leaderboard papers with no new idea.
evaluation.usefulness_criteria: A paper is useful if it changes how the reader would think or build: actionable methods, strong evidence, or ideas that transfer.
evaluation.organization: Cluster by theme, not by source. Put the most novel/important items first within each section.
